Ad Text:
MMS is embarking on a major multi‑year technology transformation, rebuilding the core digital foundations that power some of Australia’s most recognised employee benefits and salary packaging brands. We’re modernising our platforms end‑to‑end and shifting to cloud‑native, scalable, event‑driven architectures that will set the business up for the future.

As part of this journey, you’ll join a team designing and delivering modern distributed systems; re‑engineering legacy workloads into contemporary cloud patterns; and solving complex integration challenges across secure, highly available enterprise platforms. It’s an opportunity to help shape a flagship transformation and contribute to the building of solutions for millions of customers.

If you enjoy building internal platforms, automating infrastructure, and making opinionated engineering decisions that scale, this role is for you.

As a Platform Engineer, you will design and operate the cloud platform that powers our financial services, enabling engineering teams to deliver secure, reliable software from local development through to mission-critical production.

This role is for a software/cloud platform engineer working with infrastructure automation and developer platforms (not a civil, structural, or mechanical engineering role).

How you’ll make a difference

You’ll be responsible for building and evolving the cloud platform that enables secure, scalable software delivery across the organisation.

You should enjoy writing code to automate infrastructure and platforms rather than manually configuring systems.

At MMS clear expectations help our people Be the Difference.  Your key responsibilities in this role will include: 

Design, implement and maintain Infrastructure as Code using Terraform

Design, build, and maintain CI/CD pipelines as code, enabling fully automated, reliable deployments across multiple environments

Develop reusable platform components and automation to improve developer experience and consistency

Implement platform engineering practices to standardise environments from development through production

Partner with application engineering teams to improve build pipelines, deployment patterns, and platform capabilities

Embed security, governance, and automation into the platform by design

Diagnose and resolve complex issues across cloud infrastructure, platform tooling, and deployment pipelines

 To be considered for this role you will have: 

Experience building reusable Terraform modules, platform tooling, or infrastructure abstractions that enable multiple development teams

Experience working in environments where infrastructure changes are delivered through pull requests and automated pipelines

Experience building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines (not just using them)

Proven hands-on experience in platform engineering, DevOps engineering, or cloud infrastructure engineering roles

Strong experience with Azure PaaS services

Experience designing cloud-native platforms supporting multiple engineering teams

Solid understanding of DevOps practices, Git-based workflows, and automated delivery

Ability to work closely with developers to improve build, deployment, and platform capabilities

Experience embedding security, compliance, and governance into cloud platforms

 What we can offer you:

Novated leasing benefits and discounts

12 weeks paid parental leave and access to our Parents Portal

Comprehensive learning and development opportunities to support your career growth

Sonder digital wellbeing platform, providing personalised support 24/7, plus annual flu vaccinations

Default Income Protection Insurance reimbursed for members of the MMS Default Super Fund

Exempt Employee Share Plan

Embracing our value of Everyone Matters we hold a collective commitment to foster an environment where all differences are valued and respected. 

We encourage individuals from all backgrounds including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, those caring for someone or living with a disability, LGBTQIA+ and culturally diverse applicants to apply.

We embrace hybrid working and welcome conversations about flexibility. 

We value the skills and attributes veterans can bring to our organisation.    

 Please note all successful candidates will undergo background checks (including criminal history and ASIC checks) and an NDIS Workers Screening Check if appropriate.  All information provided will be treated confidentially. 

 We acknowledge Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples as the Traditional Custodians of the lands where we live, learn and work.

 If you identify as a person living with disability and require adjustments to our recruitment process, please contact us at mmsgrouprecruitment@mmsg.com.au

Job Title: Data Product Engineer (BI & AI Data Products)
Ad Text:
At our core, RSL LifeCare provides care and services to veterans and seniors who live in our communities or are receiving support at home. Our vision of creating proud communities living their best lives is at the heart of everything we do as we continue to build upon our legacy of supporting veterans and their families for more than 100 years.

This is an opportunity for you to join a large social purpose organisation where your skills and compassion will be valued and enable you to make a meaningful difference to people's lives.

We're looking for an experienced Data Product Engineer to help shape how we use data across the organisation.

In this role, you'll design, build, and maintain reliable, reusable data products that support everything from day‑to‑day reporting to advanced analytics and AI.
You'll take the lead on our curated data layer, semantic models, and enterprise KPI definitions-making sure our data is consistent, high‑quality, and easy for the business to use.
Your work will play a key part in getting the organisation ready for AI and helping us scale our analytics capability in a safe and efficient way.
You'll also collaborate closely with the BI Developer, Data Engineer and BI Developer - Insights Partner, working together to build a strong, resilient team and a data environment the whole organisation can rely on.

You will have:

Bachelor's degree in computer science, information systems, data analytics, or related field.
Strong experience in dimensional modelling (Kimball) and enterprise semantic model design.
Advanced Power BI semantic modelling skills, including DAX, KPI/measure design, and performance optimisation.
Hands‑on experience delivering curated analytics datasets using Microsoft Fabric (Warehouse/Lakehouse, Notebooks, Pipelines).
Strong SQL skills and experience implementing business transformation logic.
Experience defining and applying data quality rules for analytics and reporting.
Exposure to supporting self‑service BI through certified datasets and semantic model reuse.
Exposure to preparing datasets for AI and advanced analytics.
Ability to translate business concepts into reusable, well‑governed data products.

Desirable

Certifications in Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, or data analytics platforms.
Strong Python skills for data science use cases.
Experience with data quality monitoring, validation frameworks, or data observability.
Familiarity with data governance (classification, access controls, retention).
Experience with aged care or healthcare data, including regulated environments and clinical or funding datasets.

RSL Life Care offers the following benefits:

Not-for-profit tax benefits - salary packaging of up to $15,900 per year. This allows you to reduce your taxable income by receiving part of your salary as tax-free benefits like super contributions, car leasing, and more.
Staff discounts across 700 select retail stores through Maxxia Rewards
Fitness Passport
Leave Loading - 17.5% pay top up on paid leave

Our commitment to diversity:

We are proud to stand for our unique values: Do the Right Thing, Aim Higher, Care from the Heart, and Act as One team.

We welcome and encourage applications from people across our diverse community, especially from Veterans, current serving ADF personnel, their families and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander applicants. We believe everyone has the right to feel safe, respected, valued and heard regardless of their gender, sexual orientation, ethnicity or disability.


It is a mandatory requirement for a team member working in the Aged Care sector to have a valid Police Check or a valid National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) check, or potentially both, to undertake the inherent requirements of the role. Clearances must be verified prior to commencing employment with RSL LifeCare.

About Us

At Red Energy, we are passionate about providing award-winning customer service to over 1.5 million of our customers across the country. Owned by the mighty Snowy Hydro, we are 100% Australian owned which our customers love us for. Our award winning team have won 15 consecutive Canstar Blue Awards for best overall customer satisfaction amongst Victorian electricity providers. Ready for a new career - come grow with us!

The Role

Are you a data architect who loves building the data pipelines and transformations that power an enterprise? 

We are looking for a BI Analyst Programmer (Data Engineer) to join our Reporting & Analysis team in a fast paced retail environment. This role is for a “builder” who can design, develop, and maintain our data ecosystem. You won't just move data, you'll orchestrate and transform it across diverse environments to ensure our organization has reliable, high quality structured data for everything from high stakes regulatory compliance to real-time retail operations.

This position is designed for an experienced professional who can navigate the intricacies of both traditional relational databases and modern cloud platforms to ensure “everything flows.” Your core focus will be to partner with internal stakeholders and data specialists to translate business needs into robust, automated data workflows. You will champion data quality, adhere to established frameworks, and act as a Subject Matter Expert on SQL optimisation, indexing, and modern warehousing concepts.

What You'll Do

Reporting to the Manager of Reporting & Analysis, you will:

Architect & Orchestrate: Design, develop, and maintain scalable data pipelines (ETL/ELT) and robust data models using Star Schema, Data Vault, or Lakehouse architectures.
Bridge the Hybrid Gap: Seamlessly manage data flows between traditional relational databases (eg, SQL Server, Oracle) and modern cloud environments (eg, BigQuery, Snowflake, Databricks, Genesys Cloud).
Engineer for Performance: Partner with Data Analysts to provision low-latency, high-performance datasets optimized for visualization, analysis,  ML projects, and daily operations.
Operational Excellence: Implement version control (Git), automated quality rules, and workflow orchestration (Airflow) to ensure our data ecosystem is resilient, audit-ready, and secure.
Lead through Expertise: Act as the internal expert on SQL optimization and data design principles, mentoring the wider team to prevent reporting irregularities.

Who You Are

You are a technical specialist who views data through the lens of software engineering. You value clean code, scalable architecture, and operational stability.

The Technical Master: Mastery of SQL and proficiency in Python. You have extensive experience in data modeling, ETL/ELT, and performance tuning across hybrid platforms (GCP, Snowflake, Oracle, etc.).
The Problem Solver: You don’t just fix bugs; you identify root causes. You have a "Conceptual Thinking" mindset that transforms complex requirements into elegant technical solutions.
The Quality Champion: You have a fanatic attention to detail. You understand that "bad data in" means "bad decisions out," and you are committed to rigorous data governance.
Downstream Aware: While you focus on the backend, you have a solid understanding of BI tools (Power BI, Tableau) and how data is consumed by the end user.
The Collaborator: You are a strong communicator who can partner with source system owners and influence data design across the business.
Qualified: You hold a relevant degree (Computer Science, Math, or Data Science) and understand the commercial/regulatory drivers of the Energy sector

Why Join Us

We live by our values: Safety, Agility, Decency, Courage, Ownership, and Teamwork. You will have the autonomy to design the data structures of tomorrow while working in a supportive, high-performance culture.

Competitive employee rates for your Red Energy electricity & gas bills.
Ongoing support, development and career opportunities.
Enjoy flexible working arrangements with 3 days in the office, 2 days remote.
Exceptional 30 weeks of paid parental leave for primary carers. Plus, the option to purchase 4 weeks of additional annual leave (up to 8 weeks off every year!)

We are assessing applications as they come through so please don't hesitate and apply now!

Lead AI Engineer



Deliver AI-powered solutions that transform the experience for employees and for millions of members.
Permanent, full-time position
Flexible, hybrid working environment



At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues. We know work and life are intertwined. That's why we support the diverse needs of everyone and have policies that enable us all to thrive and be truly flexible. We ensure diversity is celebrated for the opportunity it provides us all to learn and grow and deliver better outcomes for members.



Your New Role


This role is about moving GenAI from proof‑of‑concept to trusted, enterprise‑grade systems used every day - safely, responsibly, and at scale.


As our Lead AI Engineer (internally known as Lead GenAI Systems Engineer), you'll design, build, and deliver production‑ grade AI solutions that move beyond experimentation and into real‑ world impact. You' ll lead the technical design and delivery of GenAI solutions, from LLM and RAG foundations through to agentic AI systems that orchestrate tools, workflows, and decision‑ making in production.


This is a builder‑ leader role. You' ll provide technical leadership for a small, high‑ impact team of AI engineers, while remaining deeply hands‑ on in solution design, development, and delivery. You' ll have end‑ to‑ end ownership from concept and prototyping through to production deployment, adoption, and ongoing improvement.


You'll work closely with product, data, technology, and risk partners to ensure solutions are robust, scalable, secure, and responsibly designed, aligned with enterprise standards and regulatory expectations.


If you enjoy working at the edge of what's possible with AI, this role offers both challenge and impact.



What You'll Need

You don't need to tick every box, but you'll bring strong experience across many of the following areas:



Strong hands‑ on experience building GenAI solutions, including LLM integration, prompt engineering, RAG patterns, and evaluation approaches in production environments.
Demonstrated capability in prompt design and optimisation, including structuring system and user prompts, managing context, reducing hallucinations, and improving output consistency and reliability.
Experience designing and delivering agentic AI systems, such as tool‑ using agents, workflow orchestration, multi‑ step reasoning, and human‑ in‑ the‑ loop patterns.
Hands‑ on expertise with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP), including deploying and operating AI solutions in cloud‑ based environments.
Experience with containerisation and orchestration, including Docker and Kubernetes, to support scalable, reliable AI services.
Advanced programming skills in Python and/or JavaScript, used to build, integrate, and operate GenAI and agentic systems across APIs, services, and workflows.
Demonstrated experience with MLOps / GenAI operations practices, including lifecycle management, deployment pipelines, monitoring, and operational support.
Experience embedding and operationalising Responsible AI and ethical AI principles, applying best‑ practice patterns across GenAI and agentic use cases in enterprise or regulated environments.
Strong software engineering and system design skills, with the ability to balance experimentation with production readiness, performance, cost, and maintainability.
Experience leading technical delivery, providing clear technical direction, reviewing designs and code, and supporting the development of other engineers while remaining hands‑ on.
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills, able to translate complex technical concepts for both technical and non‑ technical audiences.
An innovative, curious, and growth‑ oriented mindset, with a desire to continuously learn and apply emerging AI capabilities in practical ways.
Relevant certifications and experience in financial services or large global organisations are highly regarded.



Life at AustralianSuper


AustralianSuper is committed to colleague development, and we support our people with ongoing learning, coaching and training, as well as career opportunities across our expanding global organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and promote a blended working environment in which all roles can flex, and we're happy to discuss what this looks like for you.


We cultivate a workplace that champions safety, respect, inclusiveness and diversity. We are committed to supporting our diverse workforce in a way that is inclusive and embraces diversity in all its forms. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process or the role, please let our recruitment team know.



What's Next

If you're ready to bring your energy, integrity, generosity of spirit and passion for excellent outcomes to a team that loves a challenge, apply now!



Australian or New Zealand citizenship or Australian permanent residency status is required.



Progress powered by purpose.



AustralianSuper may use AI to review resumes and conduct initial phone interviews. This involves processing your resume, cover letter, and voice recording in Australia and the USA to help assess your suitability for the applied role. When invited to an AI phone screening, you may opt for a traditional phone screening instead. For information on how we handle your personal data, please refer to our Candidate Privacy Policy.



https://www.australiansuper.com/careers/candidate-privacy-notice

Seeking a Senior Data Engineer to join our Data and Analytics team. 

$190,026 base salary plus superannuation 

Permanent FT | Haymarket. Grow your career with us today.   

Technology: Microsoft Azure | Databricks | AI & MLOps 

 

   

About Ausgrid – we’re wired for good  

We’re the largest distributer of electricity on Australia’s east coast, which means over two million homes and businesses rely on our good work. We’re here at the heart of the energy transition, and we’re driven by a powerful purpose to make electricity accessible for all. It’s an exciting new chapter for our company with each day bringing a renewed energy to how we deliver, store and use electricity. As we continue to grow, we’re in search of exceptional talent to help us achieve our goals.   

Everyone’s job is important here. Our customers come from all walks of life, and so do we. We’re committed to fostering a diverse, respectful, and inclusive workplace, and encourage applications from all backgrounds.  

   

See yourself working here  

We’re looking for a Senior Data Engineer to play a key role in building, operating and continuously improving Ausgrid’s Enterprise Data Platform on Microsoft Azure. 

In this role, you’ll design and deliver scalable data pipelines and lakehouse solutions, support AI and MLOps capabilities, and ensure our data platform is secure, reliable and cost effective. You’ll work closely with architects, data scientists and business stakeholders to translate complex requirements into robust, production ready solutions. 

This is a hands on senior role with genuine influence over platform design, performance and operational excellence. 

This is a permanent full- time position, located at Haymarket and reports to Manager Data Services & Platforms, you will join a collaborative culture with experienced data architecture and engineering peers. 

  

Key responsibilities  

Design, build and maintain batch and streaming data pipelines using Azure Data Factory and Databricks (PySpark/Spark SQL)  

Implement Lakehouse data models (Bronze/Silver/Gold) using Delta Lake  

Support and enable AI model deployment using established MLOps / LLMOps practices  

Monitor and optimise platform performance, reliability and cost  

Apply security, governance and data privacy controls across the Azure environment  

Contribute to CI/CD pipelines and Infrastructure as Code using Bicep and Azure DevOps  

Support production workloads, including participation in a rotating 24×7 oncall roster  

Partner with stakeholders to translate business needs into scalable technical solutions  

Produce highquality technical documentation, runbooks and data dictionaries 

  

What you’ll bring   

Our hiring decisions are not only based on your experience and skills, but your passion, attitude, and alignment to our core values.   




Essential experience and skills  

Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems or similar  

Strong handson experience with Azure Data Factory, Databricks and Delta Lake  

Advanced SQL and Python / PySpark skills  

Experience working with AI models in an MLOps or similar environment  

Proven experience delivering solutions across the full development lifecycle  

Strong background supporting enterprise data platforms in production  

Experience with performance optimisation (partitioning, Zordering, caching, indexing)  

Knowledge of Azure security and governance (RBAC, Key Vault, network controls)  

Experience with DevOps practices, Git and CI/CD pipelines  

Strong stakeholder engagement skills and the ability to translate requirements into technical designs  

Comfortable participating in oncall support and incident response 

   

Life at Ausgrid   

At Ausgrid, we go beyond just a competitive salary to offer an exciting range of benefits. From salary sacrificing electric vehicles and novated leasing, to discounted corporate health insurance and access to Fitness Passport, our benefits support your work here, and your life out there.   

Celebrate success every day with our employee recognition program to bring out each other’s best. Looking to grow professionally? We’ve got you covered with study assistance, secondments, training and mentors, whose advice you’ll keep wherever you go.  We’re proud to offer gender-neutral parental leave to balance work when growing your family. And if you’re caring for elderly parents, our aged care support services offer expert guidance to help you access care for your loved ones. At Ausgrid, it's all about supporting you in every aspect of life.  

  

If you require support with your application/reasonable adjustments for the interview process, please reach out to our dedicated Talent Team recruitment@ausgrid.com.au.  

Application close date: Friday 17th April 2026 (please note, this date is subject to early closure) 

Applicants must have full Australian work rights to apply.

A leading federal government agency is seeking an AI Business Analyst to help deliver and govern responsible AI‑enabled features and services. In this role, you will work closely with multidisciplinary agile teams to translate business problems into clear, testable user stories and acceptance criteria, ensuring AI features are transparent, ethical, compliant and supported by strong human‑in‑the‑loop controls.

This position offers the opportunity to shape AI‑enabled processes, uplift organisational capability and contribute to safe, effective and scalable use of AI technologies.

Key Responsibilities

Elicit, analyse and document business, data and AI requirements; decompose into epics, features and user stories with testable acceptance criteria.
Define AI‑specific, non‑functional requirements (transparency, safety, robustness, latency, privacy, security).
Map business processes and decision points to determine AI vs rules‑based logic, including human authority levels and HITL checkpoints.
Co‑design evaluation frameworks for AI features, including datasets, baselines, performance monitoring, drift, fairness and quality.
Collaborate with data scientists, engineers and product owners to translate desired outcomes into model objectives, features and data constraints.
Lead workshops, discovery sessions and backlog refinement; produce service blueprints, journey maps and future‑state workflows.
Develop acceptance criteria and test cases for AI/ML features (shadow testing, A/B trials, red teaming, rollback plans).
Document model/prompt assumptions, guardrails, redaction rules, retrieval scope and safety constraints.
Contribute to privacy and risk assessments, including PIAs, threat assessments and governance documentation for AI artefacts.
Ensure alignment with internal AI policy, AI governance frameworks and data governance standards.
Support change management, communications and training for AI‑enabled processes and tools.
Participate in communities of practice and continuous improvement activities.

Qualifications & Experience

3+ years' experience as a Business Analyst or Systems Analyst in digital service delivery and agile environments.
Practical experience delivering or supporting AI‑enabled products within the Microsoft ecosystem (ML, NLP/LLMs, Copilot, intelligent automation).
Ability to write clear, testable user stories and acceptance criteria for AI features, including evaluation metrics and guardrails.
Knowledge of responsible and secure AI principles (privacy‑by‑design, human oversight, explainability, bias mitigation).
Strong workshop facilitation and stakeholder engagement skills with both technical and non‑technical stakeholders.
Proficiency with Azure DevOps, Miro or equivalent tools.
Excellent written and verbal communication, with an ability to influence decision‑making.

Essential Criteria

(Candidates must provide responses, up to 3000 characters each)

Demonstrated capability, knowledge, experience and clearances (if applicable) to meet role requirements.
Proven experience delivering similar AI, data or digital analysis services.
Demonstrated capacity to deliver and manage services over the proposed term.
Ability to deliver positive social or environmental impact.
Competitive pricing and value for money.
A risk profile aligned with organisational requirements.

VALD is the world leader in technology for the allied health industry, providing innovative human-measurement technology to over 10,000 clients in over 100 countries. If you have a favorite team in the NBA, EPL, or NFL, there's a good chance they use VALD Technologies.

Years on from its humble beginnings in Brisbane, Australia, VALD has grown to a team of over 300 team members in over 30 countries, with five offices across four continents.

Driven by a multidisciplinary team of researchers, clinicians, sports scientists, designers, developers and engineers, VALD’s suite of systems offer unparalleled insight into human movement, performance, injury risk and rehabilitation.

About the role

VALD is building the world’s largest musculoskeletal database, with over two billion data points and counting. Using this data, we can help practitioners to objectively assess patients’ progress and make more accurate, data-informed treatment decisions. We are looking for someone who has hybrid experience in data engineering and data analysis to help shape the future of the VALD Data Lakehouse. 

In this role, you will


Serve as a data product owner for business-facing dashboards and insights tools - ensuring they align with stakeholder needs and drive measurable impact. 

Partner with Commercial, Product, and Customer Success teams to define key metrics and build reliable, trusted datasets that power self-serve analytics and business decisions. 

Design, develop, and own dashboards that track client engagement, retention, renewals - delivering clear, actionable insights to the Commercial and Customer Success teams. 

Build and maintain scalable analytics pipelines in Azure Databricks to support client-facing and internal reporting across the commercial function. 

Apply best practices in analytics engineering and dimensional modelling to create scalable, well-documented data models for use across reporting and forecasting tools. 

Work with the business to continuously improve data generation processes across key domains including product usage, client health, and commercial performance. 

Collaborate with the Data Science team to integrate predictive models and normative benchmarks into reports and dashboards. 

What we are looking for

Our ideal candidate has: 

Has a strong passion for improving business outcomes and delivering measurable value through data  
Brings proven experience as an Analytics Engineer, building trusted, scalable data models on platforms such as Azure Databricks and Azure SQL (or similar)  
Partners effectively with stakeholders to translate business questions into clear, actionable data solutions  
Is highly proficient in Spark SQL, PySpark, and T-SQL (or similar) for transforming and modelling data to support commercial analytics use cases  
Designs and delivers high-quality semantic models and dashboards (Databricks, Power BI or similar), with a strong focus on metric definition and usability  
Has deep experience with dimensional (Kimball) data modelling to support analytics at scale  
Champions data quality, governance, and Master Data Management (MDM) to ensure consistency, accuracy, and trust across data assets 

Why VALD?

You will be working in a fast-paced, dynamic company that has a culture around the belief that anything is possible with hard work, creativity, dedication, and good timing. 

We have a range of benefits we offer to our team, such as:


The opportunity to work in a company that is redefining allied healthcare. 

Working with a range of high-performing individuals and teams across a range of disciplines. 

Working with contemporary tooling on industry first applications. 

Casual work attire. 

Chefs that prepare you delicious fresh breakfast and lunch. 

An on-site barista to make your coffee just as you like it. 

A team gym, masseuse, and access to physiotherapy and exercise physiology services. 

Diversity & Inclusion Commitment

VALD’s best asset is not our technology but our people and culture. A culture of inclusion and diversity is critical to our business. We know diverse teams perform better. It's not a separate initiative – we aim to embed inclusion and diversity in everything we do. We are committed to fostering an inclusive work environment and embracing diversity, including gender, nationality, disability, age, marital/parental status, ethnicity, gender identity, socioeconomic background and sexual orientation. We welcome applications from people from all backgrounds.

Apply now

In your cover letter, please share with us why you think your experience is relevant to this role and how this opportunity fits into your longer-term career goals. We can't wait to meet you and discuss what a career with VALD could look like for you!

Successful applicants will be subject to background checks (including identity and criminal record checks). It will be a condition of employment that the background checks return acceptable results.

Mirvac is a brand that creates and curates better experiences.

We are more than developers or builders - we are visionaries, and our ability to see the world differently drives us to be bold, embrace innovation and diversity, and lead with optimism. As creators of positive change, we see each new project as an opportunity to leave a lasting legacy, delivering enduring value for all Australians.

We focus on sustainability, innovation, and shared value, to return benefits to our customers, partners, investors, and communities. Join us as we work towards building the imagine nation.

Your Opportunity

This is a permanent opportunity to play a pivotal role in Mirvac's AI & Automation platform - core to our integrated digital operating model. You'll lead the end‑to‑end design and delivery of intelligent, agent‑oriented automation while shaping the platform roadmap and uplifting engineering standards across the business. You'll be supported by a collaborative, high‑performing team and a manager who champions innovation, trust, and continuous growth.

Your Responsibilities

Partner closely with business stakeholders to understand pain points, processes, and opportunities; craft and document AI and data use cases for inclusion in platform backlogs and prioritization discussions.
Architect and lead delivery of complex, orchestrated solutions that coordinate AI models, bots, APIs, data services, and human oversight-optimising for security, scalability, and performance.
Define and apply agentic design patterns, including confidence thresholds, escalation paths, fallback mechanisms, and recovery strategies to ensure safe and reliable automation.
Engage in forward-deployed engagements, embedding with business teams to co-design and prototype Agentic capabilities that deliver measurable value and rapid feedback loops.
Establish and enforce engineering, testing, and documentation standards, including consideration of AI failure modes, exception handling, and auditability.
Design and maintain human-in-the-loop controls, ensuring appropriate levels of autonomy, transparency, and accountability in AI-enabled processes.
Act as the primary point of contact for managed services and vendors, overseeing onboarding, delivery, ongoing management, and ensuring strong platform partnerships and vendor performance.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including product managers, data architects, and technology partners to identify and deliver high-impact opportunities aligned with strategic platform roadmaps.
Ensure compliance with enterprise security, privacy, and risk policies across all AI and Automation initiatives.
Define, track, and report metrics

Your Point of Difference

The ideal candidate will hold relevant tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or a closely related discipline, providing a strong theoretical foundation to support complex technical and architectural decision‑making.

You will also bring:

7+ years in technology roles with extensive hands-on enterprise automation experience with demonstrated technical delivery expertise (e.g. UiPath)
1-3 years' experience in using and incorporating AI/ML into workflows.
Experience with modern orchestration and agent frameworks on Azure/AWS for deploying Agentic AI solutions.
Experience building and scaling conversational AI experiences.
Proven ability to collaborate with and manage vendors and managed service providers effectively.
Solid understanding of enterprise data platforms and ability to work with data warehouses (e.g., Snowflake).

Apply today!

Your New Role

This role is about moving GenAI from proof‑of‑concept to trusted, enterprise‑grade systems used every day - safely, responsibly, and at scale.

As our Lead AI Engineer (internally known as Lead GenAI Systems Engineer), you'll design, build, and deliver production‑ grade AI solutions that move beyond experimentation and into real‑ world impact. You' ll lead the technical design and delivery of GenAI solutions, from LLM and RAG foundations through to agentic AI systems that orchestrate tools, workflows, and decision‑ making in production.

This is a builder‑ leader role. You' ll provide technical leadership for a small, high‑ impact team of AI engineers, while remaining deeply hands‑ on in solution design, development, and delivery. You' ll have end‑ to‑ end ownership from concept and prototyping through to production deployment, adoption, and ongoing improvement.

You'll work closely with product, data, technology, and risk partners to ensure solutions are robust, scalable, secure, and responsibly designed, aligned with enterprise standards and regulatory expectations.

If you enjoy working at the edge of what's possible with AI, this role offers both challenge and impact.

What You'll Need - You don't need to tick every box, but you'll bring strong experience across many of the following areas:

- Strong hands‑ on experience building GenAI solutions, including LLM integration, prompt engineering, RAG patterns, and evaluation approaches in production environments.

- Demonstrated capability in prompt design and optimisation, including structuring system and user prompts, managing context, reducing hallucinations, and improving output consistency and reliability.

- Experience designing and delivering agentic AI systems, such as tool‑ using agents, workflow orchestration, multi‑ step reasoning, and human‑ in‑ the‑ loop patterns.

- Hands‑ on expertise with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP), including deploying and operating AI solutions in cloud‑ based environments.

- Experience with containerisation and orchestration, including Docker and Kubernetes, to support scalable, reliable AI services.

- Advanced programming skills in Python and/or JavaScript, used to build, integrate, and operate GenAI and agentic systems across APIs, services, and workflows.

- Demonstrated experience with MLOps / GenAI operations practices, including lifecycle management, deployment pipelines, monitoring, and operational support.

- Experience embedding and operationalising Responsible AI and ethical AI principles, applying best‑ practice patterns across GenAI and agentic use cases in enterprise or regulated environments.

- Strong software engineering and system design skills, with the ability to balance experimentation with production readiness, performance, cost, and maintainability.

- Experience leading technical delivery, providing clear technical direction, reviewing designs and code, and supporting the development of other engineers while remaining hands‑ on.

- Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills, able to translate complex technical concepts for both technical and non‑ technical audiences.

- An innovative, curious, and growth‑ oriented mindset, with a desire to continuously learn and apply emerging AI capabilities in practical ways.

- Relevant certifications and experience in financial services or large global organisations are highly regarded.

About nxzen

nxzen is a global technology, consulting, and engineering partner supporting the energy, utilities, and national infrastructure sectors. Our work spans the full infrastructure lifecycle, partnering with clients to deliver practical, outcome-driven solutions across operations, transformation, digital platforms, and engineering. About the ROLE: Head of Data & AI

Location: Sydney

Permanent Full-time Role

The Head of Data & AI (Artificial Intelligence) will shape, build, and enable the delivery of nxzen’s Data and Artificial Intelligence proposition, with a primary focus on leveraging AI for Asset Intelligence and Operational Excellence. The role targets Utilities in Australia generally, with a particular emphasis on Australian Electricity Distribution Networks.

This role reports to the Digital CoE (Centre of Excellence) Head and collaborates closely with colleagues in the wider nxzen group. Including but not limited to other CoE teams, Client Partners, other group companies, etc. The role is both market-facing and works internally to build our capabilities in applying Artificial Intelligence to drive smarter asset management, predictive analytics, Operational Excellence, and performance improvement across utilities in Australia, with a particular focus on electricity distribution networks.

Key responsibilities for the role include: 

Strategy & Growth

Define and execute the Data & AI (Artificial Intelligence) strategy for Asset Intelligence and Operational Excellence, aligned to nxzen's business objectives and the needs of Australian utility clients.

Identify and prioritise opportunities to expand nxzen's footprint across Australian Electricity Distribution Networks and the broader utilities sector.

Work with Client Partners, clients, and NNNCo to develop go-to-market solutions and bids that combine AI-driven analytics, IoT, and asset performance improvement.

Develop compelling value propositions that demonstrate how Artificial Intelligence can enhance asset intelligence, operational efficiency, and network resilience for electricity distribution businesses.

Client & Stakeholder Engagement

Act as a senior point of contact for key utility clients, particularly Australian Electricity Distribution Networks, building trusted relationships with operations, digital, and engineering leaders.

Support Client Partners and Delivery Directors in shaping client transformation journeys that leverage AI for asset intelligence and operational excellence outcomes.

Represent nxzen externally at industry events, contributing to thought leadership and brand positioning in the Australian utilities and energy sector.

Service & Solution Development

Shape the Data & AI product and service roadmap, ensuring solutions are technically robust, scalable, and commercially competitive for the Australian electricity distribution market.

Drive the development of AI-powered solutions for asset health monitoring, predictive maintenance, risk assessment, network optimisation, and operational decision-making.

Maintain awareness of emerging technologies and trends (AI/ML, generative AI, IoT, 5G, edge computing, OT security, digital twins) and translate these into practical solutions for utility clients.

Programme Delivery Oversight

Oversee successful delivery of Data & AI programmes for utility clients, ensuring outcomes are met on quality, cost, and schedule.

Implement strong governance, assurance, and performance metrics across projects, with particular attention to the regulatory and compliance environment of Australian electricity distribution.

Ensure operational compliance with security, safety, and regulatory requirements applicable to Australian utility networks.

About YOU

Essential Knowledge

Extensive experience in Data & Artificial Intelligence, with a demonstrated ability to apply AI/ML techniques to asset intelligence and operational excellence in utility or infrastructure environments.

Deep understanding of Australian Electricity Distribution Networks, including regulatory frameworks (AER), network management challenges, and asset lifecycle management.

Proven ability to operate across IT, OT, and ET environments, integrating diverse systems to drive digital transformation and enhance asset performance using AI.

Hands-on experience managing, analysing, and deriving insights from IoT devices, OT systems, digital twins, simulation platforms, and asset health models (e.g., NARMS, CNAIM).

Demonstrated track record of leveraging AI and integrated data to inform asset performance improvement, risk assessment, and strategic decision-making.

Extensive experience applying IEC CIM, ISO 14224, and Ops 4.0 frameworks to support AI-driven asset intelligence and operational excellence initiatives.

Skilled in collaborating with cross-functional teams and stakeholders to design and deliver technically robust, scalable, and commercially viable AI-powered asset intelligence solutions.

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, Computer Science, Data Science, Information Technology, or a related discipline.

Desirable Experience

Experience selling or delivering AI and data solutions to Australian electricity distribution businesses (e.g., Ausgrid, Endeavour Energy, Essential Energy, AusNet, Powercor, SA Power Networks, Energex, Ergon Energy, Western Power, or similar).

Familiarity with Australian energy market structures, the National Electricity Rules, and the regulatory investment test (RIT-D) processes.

Experience with AI/ML platforms and tools commonly used in utility asset management and operational analytics.

Track record of building and leading high-performing teams in a consulting or professional services environment.

Professional certifications in Data & AI, IoT, or Analytics (e.g., Certified Data Scientist, AI/ML certifications, or Analytics Professional certifications).

ISO 55000 / Asset Management certification.

IEC CIM or related utility data modelling standards.

OT / Cybersecurity certifications (e.g., CISSP, CISA, or relevant OT security training).

About the role:

The Platform Security Engineer is a valued member of our engineering team. We value evidence-backed opinions and offer the opportunity to play an integral part in shaping engineering rhythm, culture, and processes across planning, architecture and design, coding, deployment, and support.


In an ever-changing environment, Lendi Group is leading the charge in developing industry-first technology designed to offer Australian homeowners transparency, simplicity, and convenience in their home loan experience.
The Platform Security Engineer partners with engineering teams to evaluate planned changes, minimise security risk, and continuously improve our security posture. A key focus of the role is driving vulnerability and security findings down over time, improving security standards, and embedding secure-by-default practices across both traditional and AI-enabled systems.

Please note: this role will be a 12 Month Fixed Term Contract

Job Description


Working closely with the Cyber Security team to ensure security principles are consistently applied across the engineering environment.
Collaborating and coding with engineering teams to uplift secure development practices and reduce recurring security findings.
Designing, building, and maintaining secure cloud and microservice solutions.
Improving security posture through effective use of COTS products, platform controls, and network infrastructure.
Vulnerability and Threat Management — monitoring and assessing assets including cloud infrastructure, containers, packages, APIs, and requests, with a focus on remediation and measurable risk reduction.
Supporting the secure design and operation of AI-enabled systems, including LLM integrations, automation agents, and MCP-based architectures.
Implementing and reviewing AI security guardrails such as access controls, data protection, prompt-injection mitigation, and output validation.
Collaborating with cross-functional teams including engineering, operations, compliance, data, and AI platform teams.
Contributing as an individual contributor within a cross-functional DevOps / SecOps team.
Build or integrate services to improve security and compliance

Qualifications


The ideal candidate is passionate about promoting a security-first culture and has a strong, current understanding of security best practices, with particular focus on cloud security, secure design, incident handling, and operational resilience.
Experience securing microservices in cloud environments and working with configuration-as-code is important for this role. Experience securing AI-driven systems, including LLM-powered services and internal automation, is highly regarded.
Strong communication skills are essential. We value engineers who can clearly communicate risk, trade-offs, and remediation paths, and who approach collaboration with empathy for engineering challenges.

Key Skills

Strong understanding of networking, infrastructure, and applications from a DevOps perspective with a security focus.
Hands-on experience implementing and maintaining security controls across cloud environments (particularly AWS) and access management solutions (e.g. Auth0).
Experience with security logging, monitoring, and vulnerability assessment, with a demonstrated ability to drive findings down and improve baseline security standards.
Passion for secure coding practices and identifying common implementation and operational security risks.

Desirable Experience

AWS technologies such as CloudFormation, Terraform, IAM, WAF, Node.js and/or TypeScript.
Docker, containerised environments, serverless architectures, and Kubernetes.
Event-driven systems.
Securing AI platforms, including LLM integrations, MCP implementations, AI guardrails, and automation/build agents.

Your primary responsibilities include leading and mentoring your team, designing and implementing advanced machine learning and statistical models, translating complex business problems into scalable data products, and leading the end-to-end lifecycle from discovery to production. You will lead by example with modern data science techniques and tooling, providing clear technical direction and coaching to team members. 

We’re looking for a collaborative team player who thrives in a dynamic environment, embraces the challenge of legacy systems, remains composed under pressure, and is driven to deliver continuous improvement and strategic impact.  This role provides a unique opportunity to connect directly with our stakeholders.  You should be a versatile data leader who can both drive hands-on delivery and lead the development of new data initiatives—balancing strategic oversight with practical execution. 

Job Description


On a typical day you will: 

Lead, mentor and motivate a data science team (up to10 people) and set the technical direction and standards 
Collaborate within a cross-functional digital team, including Product Management, Engineering and Design. 
Bridge the gap between business problems and data science solutions, turning opportunities into impactful, production-ready models. 
Utilise strong analytical and modelling skills to design, build, and validate machine learning solutions, particularly tree-based methods (e.g. XGBoost, GBM, LightGBM), neural networks, and transformer-based approaches. 
Define and lead the end-to-end lifecycle of data products in an agile environment, from problem framing and experimentation through to deployment and monitoring. 
Conduct and document detailed analysis, including business case development, to prioritise use cases and clearly communicate trade-offs and expected value to stakeholders. 
Work closely with data and platform engineers on ETL pipelines, containerised solutions (e.g. Docker, Kubernetes), and cloud-based environments (e.g. GCP, AWS, Azure), with exposure to modern data platforms such as Snowflake and Databricks. 
Collaborate with cross-functional Agile teams (Engineering, Product, Design, QA) in remote and on-site settings, applying appropriate governance and engagement models to data projects. 
Provide BAU support for existing models and data products, while actively managing demand, prioritisation, and stakeholder expectations to create space for strategic, high-impact data science initiatives. 

Qualifications


Proven end-to-end Data Science experience (6+ years), including recent experience operating at Senior/Lead level 
Proven ability to lead, mentor and motivate a data science team (typically 3–10 people) and to set technical direction and standards 
Strong, hands-on expertise with modern machine learning models and algorithms, particularly tree-based methods (e.g. XGBoost, GBM, LightGBM), statistical models, and neural networks 
Demonstrated experience working with LLMs, transformers and embeddings in practical, production-oriented use cases 
Solid understanding of the end-to-end lifecycle of data products, from problem framing and model development through to productisation, pipelining and BAU support 
Proficiency in Python and a strong command of SQL for data exploration, feature engineering and model implementation 
Experience working with cloud platforms (e.g. GCP, AWS or Azure) and familiarity with modern data ecosystems such as Snowflake and/or Databricks (or ability to upskill quickly) 
Exposure to containerised solutions (e.g. Docker) and collaborating with engineers on production deployment and MLOps practices 
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to translate business problems into data solutions and tell a compelling story with insights 
Experience contributing to or leading governance and engagement models (e.g. steering committees, RACI/RASCI frameworks, working groups) across multiple stakeholders 
Demonstrated capability in prioritisation and managing tradeoffs in a resourceconstrained or scaleup environment, including pushing back on lowvalue work 
Experience developing or contributing to business cases for data and AI initiatives, including articulating value, risks and assumptions
